suggestions.title: Memoize expensive filter/map operations in App.jsx render
suggestions.description: activeWorkers is re-computed every render as a plain filter, then getTeamVibe(activeWorkers) is called 3+ times in the JSX (StatsBar, header vibe pill, inline text). Wrap activeWorkers in useMemo and compute vibe once. The workers.filter(w => w.status === 'working').length inside JSX also re-runs on every render. This matters because the 30s polling causes re-renders even when data hasn't changed.

suggestions.title: useEffect dependency on activeWorkers causes infinite re-render risk
suggestions.description: The cinematic entry/exit useEffect depends on [activeWorkers], but activeWorkers is a new array reference every render (created via .filter()). This means the effect fires on EVERY render, not just when workers actually change. Should use useMemo for activeWorkers or compare by value (e.g., JSON.stringify or by checking worker IDs).

suggestions.title: No loading/error state differentiation between tabs
suggestions.description: The Office tab has no loading skeleton or error state — if the fetch fails, Office just shows an empty floor with ghost characters and no indication anything went wrong. The error/warning banners only render inside the dashboard tab's parent but are actually in the main section. Move error banners above the tab toggle so they're visible on both tabs.

suggestions.title: CharacterAvatar component is 200+ lines of inline SVG — extract to separate file
suggestions.description: Office.jsx is ~500 lines, with CharacterAvatar taking up nearly half. Each character (bloberto, carlos, maya, dave, sofia, luna) has hand-drawn SVG inline. Extract to CharacterAvatar.jsx or even individual character SVG components. Makes Office.jsx readable and characters independently testable.

suggestions.title: Tab panel missing aria-labelledby and tab IDs
suggestions.description: The tab buttons have aria-controls pointing to tabpanel IDs, but the buttons themselves lack id attributes. For proper ARIA tab pattern, each tab button needs an id, and each tabpanel needs aria-labelledby pointing back. Also, inactive tab panels should be hidden (hidden attribute or display:none) rather than just not rendered — screen readers may announce the tabpanel container.

suggestions.title: Activity log re-reverses and re-slices on every render
suggestions.description: In ActivityLog, [...entries].reverse().slice(0, 20) creates two new arrays every render. Since entries can grow large over time, memoize this. Also the filter in the parent (activityLog.filter) runs before passing to ActivityLog, then ActivityLog reverses — the filter should be inside the memoization.

suggestions.title: Office window time is based on viewer's local clock, not team timezone
suggestions.description: WindowElement uses new Date().getHours() which is the viewer's local time. If someone views from Tokyo while the team is in PST, the window shows Tokyo's sky. Consider using a fixed timezone (PST) or making it configurable, since the office is 'Bloberto's HQ' which has a canonical location.

suggestions.title: No way to see completed/done workers or historical data
suggestions.description: The dashboard only shows active workers (working/idle/error). There's no archive or 'recently completed' section. The activity log partially covers this, but a 'Recent Completions' accordion or section showing the last 5 completed workers with their final task would add useful context without cluttering the UI.

suggestions.title: Stats bar vibe card text can overflow on mobile
suggestions.description: The vibe-value has white-space: nowrap and font-size 1rem, but labels like 'Crushing It' or 'After Hours' can overflow the stat-card on narrow screens (2-column grid at 600px). Add text overflow handling or reduce font size at small breakpoints.

suggestions.title: ROLE_EMOJIS duplicated between App.jsx and Office.jsx
suggestions.description: App.jsx has ROLE_EMOJIS map for worker cards, Office.jsx has ROLE_COLORS and DEFAULT_ROSTER. These role-related constants should live in a shared constants file (e.g., src/utils/constants.js) to avoid drift when adding new roles.

suggestions.title: Add a simple search/filter for workers in dashboard view
suggestions.description: With a growing team, being able to filter workers by name or role in the dashboard tab would be useful. A small search input above the workers grid. Low priority since team is small now, but good to have the pattern ready.

suggestions.title: Office plants lack variety — all three use similar green palettes
suggestions.description: The three decorative plants are nice but visually samey. The cactus is slightly different but still green-on-brown. Consider giving one a flower, or varying pot colors more. Purely cosmetic but adds to the fun factor.
